# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

variables:
  DEPLOY_TAG_PREFIX: "deploy"
  RELEASE_NOTES_FILE: "RELEASE_NOTES.md"

stages:
  - validate
  - build
  - test
  - deploy
  - notify

validate_mr:
  stage: validate
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
  script:
    - echo "Validating merge request metadata"
    - |
      if [ -z "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        echo "No merge request IID found, skipping MR validation"
        exit 0
      fi
    - echo "MR IID: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID"
    - echo "Processing merge request !${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}"

build:
  stage: build
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  script:
    - echo "Building project for commit ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    - |
      # Write MR metadata to a file to avoid shell injection
      # Never interpolate MR title/description directly into shell commands
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        # Use printf with %s to safely handle special characters
        printf '%s\n' "BUILD_MR_IID=${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}" > build_metadata.env
        printf '%s\n' "BUILD_COMMIT_SHA=${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}" >> build_metadata.env
        printf '%s\n' "BUILD_PIPELINE_ID=${CI_PIPELINE_ID}" >> build_metadata.env
      fi
    - echo "Build completed successfully"
  artifacts:
    reports:
      dotenv: build_metadata.env
    paths:
      - build_metadata.env
    expire_in: 1 hour

test:
  stage: test
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"'
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  script:
    - echo "Running tests for commit ${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    - echo "Tests passed"

generate_release_notes:
  stage: deploy
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  script:
    - |
      echo "Generating release notes for pipeline ${CI_PIPELINE_ID}"

      # Safely write release notes header
      printf '# Release Notes - Pipeline %s\n\n' "$CI_PIPELINE_ID" > "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
      printf 'Date: %s\n' "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
      printf 'Commit: %s\n\n' "$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"

      # Use GitLab API to safely retrieve MR information instead of using
      # user-controlled CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE / CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION
      # which could contain shell metacharacters or injection payloads
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        echo "## Changes from MR !${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
        echo "" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"

        # Fetch MR details via API and use jq to safely extract fields
        # This avoids any shell injection from user-controlled MR title/description
        MR_JSON=$(curl --silent --fail --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}" \
          "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/merge_requests/${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}")

        if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -n "$MR_JSON" ]; then
          # jq -r safely extracts and outputs the raw string without shell interpretation
          SAFE_TITLE=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '.title // "No title"')
          SAFE_DESCRIPTION=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '.description // "No description"')
          SAFE_AUTHOR=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '.author.username // "unknown"')
          SAFE_LABELS=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '(.labels // []) | join(", ")')

          # Use printf %s to write without interpreting escape sequences or special chars
          printf '### %s\n\n' "$SAFE_TITLE" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
          printf 'Author: @%s\n' "$SAFE_AUTHOR" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
          if [ -n "$SAFE_LABELS" ]; then
            printf 'Labels: %s\n' "$SAFE_LABELS" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
          fi
          printf '\n#### Description\n\n%s\n\n' "$SAFE_DESCRIPTION" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
        else
          echo "Could not fetch MR details from API" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
        fi
      else
        echo "## Direct commit to main" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
        echo "" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
        # Use git log with format to safely get commit message
        # --format is safe as it reads from git objects, not user shell input
        git log -1 --format='%s' "$CI_COMMIT_SHA" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
        echo "" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
      fi

      echo "---" >> "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
      cat "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
    expire_in: 30 days

create_deployment_tag:
  stage: deploy
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  script:
    - |
      echo "Creating deployment tag"

      # Generate a safe tag name using only controlled/sanitized values
      TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
      TAG_NAME="${DEPLOY_TAG_PREFIX}-${TIMESTAMP}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"

      echo "Tag name: ${TAG_NAME}"

      # Build tag message safely by fetching MR data via API
      TAG_MESSAGE_FILE=$(mktemp)

      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        MR_JSON=$(curl --silent --fail --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}" \
          "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/merge_requests/${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}")

        if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -n "$MR_JSON" ]; then
          SAFE_TITLE=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '.title // "No title"')
          # Write tag message to a file to avoid any shell injection
          printf 'Deployment: %s\nMR: !%s\nTitle: %s\nPipeline: %s\n' \
            "$TAG_NAME" "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" "$SAFE_TITLE" "$CI_PIPELINE_ID" > "$TAG_MESSAGE_FILE"
        else
          printf 'Deployment: %s\nCommit: %s\nPipeline: %s\n' \
            "$TAG_NAME" "$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA" "$CI_PIPELINE_ID" > "$TAG_MESSAGE_FILE"
        fi
      else
        printf 'Deployment: %s\nCommit: %s\nPipeline: %s\n' \
          "$TAG_NAME" "$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA" "$CI_PIPELINE_ID" > "$TAG_MESSAGE_FILE"
      fi

      # Configure git safely
      git config user.email "ci-bot@${CI_SERVER_HOST}"
      git config user.name "CI Bot"

      # Use -F to read message from file, avoiding shell interpretation entirely
      git tag -a "$TAG_NAME" -F "$TAG_MESSAGE_FILE" "$CI_COMMIT_SHA"

      # Push using CI job token (scoped, short-lived credential)
      git push "https://gitlab-ci-token:${CI_JOB_TOKEN}@${CI_SERVER_HOST}/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}.git" "$TAG_NAME"

      rm -f "$TAG_MESSAGE_FILE"

      echo "DEPLOY_TAG=${TAG_NAME}" >> deploy.env
  artifacts:
    reports:
      dotenv: deploy.env
    expire_in: 1 hour

deploy_production:
  stage: deploy
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
      when: manual
  needs:
    - job: create_deployment_tag
      artifacts: true
    - job: generate_release_notes
      artifacts: true
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://production.example.com
  script:
    - echo "Deploying tag ${DEPLOY_TAG} to production"
    - echo "Deployment completed"

notify_slack:
  stage: notify
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  needs:
    - job: create_deployment_tag
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - |
      echo "Sending deployment notification"

      # Build notification payload safely using jq to construct JSON
      # jq --arg safely handles special characters in values (quotes, newlines, etc.)
      # This prevents JSON injection from user-controlled MR fields

      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        MR_JSON=$(curl --silent --fail --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: ${GITLAB_API_TOKEN}" \
          "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/merge_requests/${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}")

        if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -n "$MR_JSON" ]; then
          SAFE_TITLE=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '.title // "No title"')
          SAFE_AUTHOR=$(echo "$MR_JSON" | jq -r '.author.username // "unknown"')
        else
          SAFE_TITLE="Direct commit"
          SAFE_AUTHOR="unknown"
        fi
      else
        SAFE_TITLE="Direct commit to main"
        SAFE_AUTHOR="unknown"
      fi

      # Use jq to safely construct the JSON payload - this properly escapes all values
      PAYLOAD=$(jq -n \
        --arg title "$SAFE_TITLE" \
        --arg tag "${DEPLOY_TAG:-unknown}" \
        --arg project "$CI_PROJECT_NAME" \
        --arg pipeline "$CI_PIPELINE_URL" \
        --arg author "$SAFE_AUTHOR" \
        --arg mr_iid "${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID:-N/A}" \
        '{
          text: ("🚀 Deployment Notification\n*Project:* " + $project + "\n*Tag:* `" + $tag + "`\n*MR:* !" + $mr_iid + " - " + $title + "\n*Author:* @" + $author + "\n*Pipeline:* " + $pipeline)
        }')

      if [ -n "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
        curl --silent --fail -X POST \
          -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
          --data "$PAYLOAD" \
          "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"
        echo "Slack notification sent"
      else
        echo "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL not configured, skipping notification"
        echo "Would have sent: $PAYLOAD"
      fi

notify_email:
  stage: notify
  rules:
    - if: '$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"'
  needs:
    - job: generate_release_notes
      artifacts: true
    - job: create_deployment_tag
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - |
      echo "Generating email notification content"

      # Read release notes from the file artifact (already safely generated)
      if [ -f "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE" ]; then
        echo "Release notes available:"
        cat "$RELEASE_NOTES_FILE"
      fi

      echo "Email notification would be sent to deployment mailing list"
      echo "Deploy tag: ${DEPLOY_TAG:-unknown}"
      echo "Pipeline: ${CI_PIPELINE_URL}"
